before: Eggs Benedict and the Reuben Patty Melt are standouts. Short rib hash worth ordering. Good Scottsdale brunch.
after: Heard the Eggs Benedict and the Reuben Patty Melt are the standouts, and the short rib hash gets a shout too. Supposed to be a good Scottsdale brunch.

before: Festive atmosphere. Made-from-scratch Mexican with good margaritas.
after: Festive atmosphere. Made-from-scratch Mexican — the margaritas get good word of mouth.

before: Fine dining with live music. Top-quality steaks in a classy setting.
after: Fine dining with live music. Steaks are billed as top quality in a classy setting.

before: Regional Mexican specialties in a bright pink building. Feels like eating at grandma's. Menu is pricey and portions are small — go for the experience.
after: Regional Mexican specialties in a bright pink building. Reviews say it feels like eating at grandma's. Menu is pricey and portions run small — go for the experience, they say.

before: Philly cheese steak sub is a must try. Wings and thin crust pizza also great. Baseball-themed neighborhood spot.
after: Word is the Philly cheese steak sub is the must-try, with the wings and thin crust pizza right behind. Baseball-themed neighborhood spot.

before: Tequila and mezcal bar downtown PHX. Massive agave selection — even their well stuff is good. Food truck out front with solid tacos. $7 beer and shot at happy hour.
after: Tequila and mezcal bar downtown PHX. Massive agave selection — supposedly even their well stuff is good. Food truck out front with tacos people vouch for. $7 beer and shot at happy hour.

before: More creative than most Wichita BBQ. Tasty and different.
after: Supposed to be more creative than most Wichita BBQ — tasty and different, per the word around town.

before: Cacio e pepe in the cheese wheel. Housemade pasta. Looks unassuming from the outside, turns out to be elegant inside.
after: Cacio e pepe in the cheese wheel. Housemade pasta. Looks unassuming from the outside — word is it's elegant inside.

before: Cookie butter cheesecake is the move. Supposed to rival New York-level cheesecake. Worth the drive from anywhere in the valley.
after: People swear by the cookie butter cheesecake — supposed to rival New York-level cheesecake and worth the drive from anywhere in the valley.

before: Classic Mexican-American breakfast spot. Chorizo skillet is big and delicious — ask for refried beans added. Red chili con carne is fresh, not from a can.
after: Classic Mexican-American breakfast spot. The chorizo skillet is supposedly big and delicious — tip is to ask for refried beans added. Red chili con carne is said to be fresh, not from a can.

before: Retro vibe smash burgers — red neon, 80s music, skylight. Waffle fries are great. Lettuce wrap instead of a bun is available. One of the better burger spots in Uptown.
after: Retro vibe smash burgers — red neon, 80s music, skylight. Reviews rave about the waffle fries. Lettuce wrap instead of a bun is available. Heard it's one of the better burger spots in Uptown.

before: Corner store with a Chinese kitchen inside. Buffalo chicken wings and the egg rolls are better than most actual Chinese restaurants. Been around since 1988. Shrimp fried rice is the classic order.
after: Corner store with a Chinese kitchen inside. Word of mouth says the buffalo chicken wings and egg rolls are better than most actual Chinese restaurants. Been around since 1988. Shrimp fried rice is the classic order.

before: Hawaiian plate lunch spot that's been around since the 90s. Kalbi plate and the poke are the highlights. Jr Special is the go-to order.
after: Hawaiian plate lunch spot that's been around since the 90s. Heard the kalbi plate and the poke are the highlights, and the Jr Special is the go-to order.

before: The Cuban sandwich hits. Also get the German potato salad — cold. Turkey cranberry sandwich is solid too.
after: Reviews rave about the Cuban sandwich. The German potato salad — served cold — gets called out too, and the turkey cranberry sandwich is supposed to be solid.

before: Set inside a restored old church with stained glass windows and vaulted ceilings — the space is wild. Sustainable Mexican fare, great habanero salsa. Strong drinks. Craft beer and tequila program.
after: Set inside a restored old church with stained glass windows and vaulted ceilings — the space looks wild. Sustainable Mexican fare; people rave about the habanero salsa. Supposedly strong drinks. Craft beer and tequila program.

before: Shrimp taco is the standout when they have it. Asada burrito is solid and filling. Salsa bar is the move. Quality has fluctuated but on a good day it hits.
after: Heard the shrimp taco is the standout when they have it. Asada burrito supposedly solid and filling, and regulars say the salsa bar is the move. Reviews say quality has fluctuated but on a good day it hits.

before: Creative gourmet sandwiches with seasonal ingredients. The chicken pesto caprese is a winner. Busy at dinner — online order might run long.
after: Creative gourmet sandwiches with seasonal ingredients. Word is the chicken pesto caprese is a winner. Supposedly busy at dinner — online order might run long.

before: Japanese-style crepes. Self-serve ordering kiosks inside. The Angus short rib crepe is one of the more interesting options. Mango crepe is huge and stuffed.
after: Japanese-style crepes. Self-serve ordering kiosks inside. The Angus short rib crepe looks like one of the more interesting options. Mango crepe is reportedly huge and stuffed.

before: Scottsdale brewery doing more traditional styles — Czech Amber Lager and Dark Lager are the sleepers. Beerded Barbecue food truck on-site daily. Big patio.
after: Scottsdale brewery doing more traditional styles — beer people say the Czech Amber Lager and Dark Lager are the sleepers. Beerded Barbecue food truck on-site daily. Big patio.

before: Home-style Punjabi. Tarka dal and butter chicken are solid. Malai kofta is apparently the best version around here. Order medium spice — it runs hot.
after: Home-style Punjabi. Reviews rave about the tarka dal and butter chicken, and the malai kofta is apparently the best version around here. Heard to order medium spice — it runs hot.

before: South Indian style — different from the typical valley Indian spots. Gongura Chicken Curry is the standout. Kadai Chicken is good too.
after: South Indian style — different from the typical valley Indian spots. The Gongura Chicken Curry gets called the standout. Kadai Chicken supposedly good too.

before: Best pho in Arizona according to a lot of people. Huge portions, no upcharge to substitute. Spring rolls are legit — rice paper doesn't fall apart. Small parking lot.
after: Best pho in Arizona according to a lot of people. Huge portions, no upcharge to substitute. Reviewers swear the spring rolls are legit — rice paper doesn't fall apart. Small parking lot.

before: The Sonoran Philly is the order. Sweet potato fries are great. French Dip is also solid. Local spot that doesn't try to be anything it's not.
after: Everyone says the Sonoran Philly is the order. Sweet potato fries supposedly great, French Dip solid too per reviews. Local spot that doesn't try to be anything it's not.

before: 100% vegan smash burgers that fool meat eaters. Chicken ranch wrap might be better than the burgers. Fries are impossible to resist. A little pricey but quality is obvious.
after: 100% vegan smash burgers that supposedly fool meat eaters. Heard the chicken ranch wrap might be better than the burgers, and people say the fries are impossible to resist. A little pricey but reviews say the quality is obvious.

before: Yelp's #6 taco spot in the US. The Sonoran taco is the one to get. Solid salsa bar with pico, cabbage, carrots, lime, and atolía. Aqua fresca is also good.
after: Yelp's #6 taco spot in the US. The Sonoran taco is supposedly the one to get. Salsa bar with pico, cabbage, carrots, lime, and atolía sounds solid. Aqua fresca gets mentions too.

before: Speakeasy taco spot tucked in an alley off Central, easy access from the light rail. Quesadilla is the move — better than the tacos. $5 beers, big outdoor seating area.

before: Mexican-Indian fusion. Shrimp coconut curry and the paneer biryani are the standouts. Tamarind sauce with samosas is a must. The shrimp tacos use fresh ingredients and it shows.

before: Best tortillas in town according to regulars. Green pork tamales are full of flavor. Conchas are solid. The cream horn is a sleeper hit.

before: Superhero-themed burger spot with arcade games playing Marvel and DC movies. Chili cheese fries are a solid value. Fun for kids, food is decent.

before: Chips and souiza to start. Chipotle chicken enchiladas with the house sauce are over the top. Chile relleno is cooked right. Local spot that feels like you're eating somewhere only locals know.

before: Ranked third in the nation for Mediterranean in 2024. Shawarma is the main event — juicy, well-seasoned. Get the fries extra crispy. Huge portions, bring people. The Tractor lemonade is also good.

before: Latin cafe with a walk-up patio. Fish tacos are the thing people come back for. Abuelita mocha is solid. Pastelitos and chilaquiles are also crowd favorites.

after: Upscale cocktail bar with live entertainment. Oysters and steak for dinner if you want to commit. Happy hour sounds great — reportedly $15 on a $40 scotch drink. People swear the glam vibe actually delivers in person.

before: Neighborhood bar with solid burgers and a good Saturday brunch 10am-2pm. Fish specials rotate — barramundi when they have it. Pastrami burger is the main draw.

before: Downtown Phoenix cheap eats institution. Triple burger is $5.45. Coneys with chili are the classic move — eat fast or the chili makes them soggy. Cheesy chili tots are also great.
after: Downtown Phoenix cheap eats institution. Triple burger is $5.45. Coneys with chili are the classic move — people say eat fast or the chili makes them soggy. Cheesy chili tots supposedly great too.

before: Filipino restaurant and mini mart. The lumpia is apparently worth going back for the next day — someone bought 50 more. Lechon is also on point. Small and clean.

after: Chinese food. Peking duck with rolls looks great. Heard the wonton soup is the one to get.

before: Great wings

before: Dive bar with pool tables inside and on the patio. Low beer prices at happy hour. Good stop on the way to Cave Creek.
after: Dive bar with pool tables inside and on the patio. Low beer prices at happy hour. Looks like a good stop on the way to Cave Creek.

before: Green chile pork burrito is the signature. Carne asada burrito with rice and beans also excellent. Melrose District staple.
after: Green chile pork burrito is the signature. The carne asada burrito with rice and beans also gets big praise. Melrose District staple.

before: Good chicken wings
after: Known for good chicken wings.

before: Speakeasy-style spot with New Mexican smoked meats and a rooftop patio. The meatloaf and shepherd's pie are standouts. Good craft beer selection.
after: Speakeasy-style spot with New Mexican smoked meats and a rooftop patio. The meatloaf and shepherd's pie are the reported standouts. Craft beer selection is supposed to be good.

before: Locally sourced American fare in a big industrial space. Grilled chicken wings to start, ribeye and ribs for the table.
after: Locally sourced American fare in a big industrial space. On the list for grilled chicken wings to start, ribeye and ribs for the table.

before: All vegan comfort food. The buffalo wings and ranch are the standout. Po boy and cheesesteak sandwich also popular.
after: All vegan comfort food. The buffalo wings and ranch get called the standout. Po boy and cheesesteak sandwich also popular.

before: Classic local Mexican chain. Shredded chicken tinga burrito and good thin chips. Nothing groundbreaking but solid and consistent.
after: Classic local Mexican chain. Shredded chicken tinga burrito and reportedly good thin chips. Rep is nothing groundbreaking but solid and consistent.

before: This thriving enterprise produces those giant Mexican sammies known as tortas. These are the Godzillas of the sandwich world, and could easily kick any ciabatta's butt, then go on to feed a family of six. The Torta del Rey is like a Mexican Dagwood, with fried beef, ham, egg, chorizo, avocado, and about a half-dozen more items, grilled and stacked high in a soft Mexican bread known as a telera. Los Reyes de la Torta also boasts a juice bar with all types of fresh-squeezed aguas frescas - lime, pineapple, mango, and so on. Or you can have six or more juices together in a colossal drink called a bomba, or add to the decadence level with a fruit and condensed milk creation called an agua cremosa. All of the fruit drinks come topped with little paper umbrellas, rounding out this colorful experience. ($)
after: Known for giant Mexican sammies called tortas — reviews call them the Godzillas of the sandwich world, supposedly able to kick any ciabatta's butt and still feed a family of six. The Torta del Rey is described as a Mexican Dagwood: fried beef, ham, egg, chorizo, avocado, and about a half-dozen more items, grilled and stacked high in a soft Mexican bread known as a telera. Also boasts a juice bar with all types of fresh-squeezed aguas frescas — lime, pineapple, mango, and so on. You can get six or more juices together in a colossal drink called a bomba, or add to the decadence with a fruit and condensed milk creation called an agua cremosa. All the fruit drinks come topped with little paper umbrellas. ($)

before: The ambiance of Tia Carmen is intentional in its efforts to honor the Southwest region and Mexico. As you walk through the large wooden doors, you're greeted by a down-to-earth vibe and quiet elegance. Chef Angelo Sosa wanted to recreate a reminder of his experience with his Aunt Carmen when he first learned food was his love language. The menu is not only a homage to his roots and to indigenous people but also a festival of culinary delights. Highlights include ember-roasted purple yam, chicken guisado, Baja striped bass, yucca brulee, and native grain fried rice. Ingredients are sourced locally, which reflects Sosa's desire to support the community. The entrees are complemented with craft cocktails, including the gin-based Hibiscus Desert Balloon, a yuzu and bergamot Paloma, and Mi Tia, a tropical drink that honors Sosa's family. ($$$)
after: Supposed to honor the Southwest and Mexico in everything down to the ambiance — reviews describe a down-to-earth vibe and quiet elegance once you're through the large wooden doors. Chef Angelo Sosa built it as a reminder of his experience with his Aunt Carmen, when he first learned food was his love language, and the menu is a homage to his roots and to indigenous people. Highlights people point to: ember-roasted purple yam, chicken guisado, Baja striped bass, yucca brulee, and native grain fried rice. Ingredients are sourced locally, reflecting Sosa's desire to support the community. Craft cocktails too, including the gin-based Hibiscus Desert Balloon, a yuzu and bergamot Paloma, and Mi Tia, a tropical drink that honors Sosa's family. ($$$)

before: Tiny 1945 diner with counter seating. Welcome burger and pancakes are the go-tos. Street parking only.
after: Tiny 1945 diner with counter seating. Welcome burger and pancakes are supposedly the go-tos. Street parking only.

before: Legit NY-style thin crust slices. Good sauce and cheese ratio. Foldable. Add garlic if you're into it.
after: By all accounts legit NY-style thin crust slices with a good sauce and cheese ratio. Foldable, they say. Add garlic if you're into it.

before: Potato tacos are perfectly spicy. Al pastor burrito is also a standout. Pool table, good beer selection.
after: People say the potato tacos are perfectly spicy and the al pastor burrito is a standout too. Pool table, good beer selection.

before: Rose chai latte is excellent. Brunch spot with a Yelp waitlist — expect a wait on weekends.
after: The rose chai latte gets rave reviews. Brunch spot with a Yelp waitlist — expect a wait on weekends.

before: Best view in Phoenix. Food is OK, better for drinks
after: Supposedly the best view in Phoenix. Word is the food is OK, better for drinks

before: Really good sour dough
after: People swear by the sour dough

before: Mac and cheese pizza and truffle fries are the standouts. Buffalo mac is also excellent. Counter order, they bring it to you.
after: Heard the mac and cheese pizza and truffle fries are the standouts, and the buffalo mac gets raves too. Counter order, they bring it to you.

before: The Titanic hot dog is the one to get. Also known for the Ruffles hot dog with chips on top. Loaded fries and nachos available.
after: Word is the Titanic hot dog is the one to get. Also known for the Ruffles hot dog with chips on top. Loaded fries and nachos available.

before: Farm setting with tree-shaded picnic tables. Japanese sweet potato is a must. French toast is also a good pick.
after: Farm setting with tree-shaded picnic tables. Reviews say the Japanese sweet potato is a must and the French toast a good pick too.

before: Best pho in phx has a dessert place next door
after: Supposed to be the best pho in phx, has a dessert place next door

before: Great name, great BBQ. Competition-style smoked meats.
after: Great name, and people say the BBQ lives up to it. Competition-style smoked meats.

before: French and Mediterranean in a historic redbrick cottage. The bouillabaisse and filet are both excellent. Gin cocktails are standouts.
after: French and Mediterranean in a historic redbrick cottage. Reviews rave about the bouillabaisse and filet, and the gin cocktails get called standouts.

before: NY-style bagels. The morning medic (Taylor ham, egg, cheese on a kaiser roll) is the move. Proper cream cheese amounts, bagels sliced the right way.
after: NY-style bagels. Heard the morning medic (Taylor ham, egg, cheese on a kaiser roll) is the move — proper cream cheese amounts, bagels sliced the right way.

before: People claim it's the best birria tacos in Phoenix. Been around 20 years. Quesobirria is the move, beans and rice taste homemade.
after: People claim it's the best birria tacos in Phoenix. Been around 20 years. Word is the quesobirria is the move and the beans and rice taste homemade.

before: Food truck hibachi. Huge portions, fresh, spicy — the fried rice alone is worth it. Great value.
after: Food truck hibachi. Reviews rave about huge portions, fresh and spicy — the fried rice alone supposedly worth it. Great value by all accounts.

before: Fine dining in a secluded house with cocktails. The cocktail list is serious — about 40 options. Good date spot.
after: Fine dining in a secluded house with cocktails. The cocktail list runs about 40 options. Supposed to be a good date spot.

before: Homemade pasta and pizza dough. Calamari to start, then anything on the pasta menu. Big portions, worth the drive.
after: Homemade pasta and pizza dough. Word is calamari to start, then anything on the pasta menu. Big portions, supposedly worth the drive.

before: Chilaquiles and albondigas soup are both solid. Long-running family spot — been around decades. Entrance is kind of hidden.
after: Heard the chilaquiles and albondigas soup are both solid. Long-running family spot — been around decades. Entrance is kind of hidden.

before: From the owners of Los chicken and waffles, and Monroe's hot chicken. Pancakes are fluffy and legit. Lobster with creole shrimp. Expect a long wait on weekends.
after: From the owners of Los chicken and waffles, and Monroe's hot chicken. People swear the pancakes are fluffy and legit. Lobster with creole shrimp. Expect a long wait on weekends.

before: Indian-style pizza in Tempe. Curry tacos are the move. Paneer with jalapeños is a must-try. Genuinely creative menu.
after: Indian-style pizza in Tempe. The curry tacos are supposedly the move, paneer with jalapeños a must-try. Genuinely creative menu.

before: Best burgers and BBQ in Norwich. Famous BBQ Burger plus lobster alfredo and chicken parm.
after: Locals call it the best burgers and BBQ in Norwich. Famous BBQ Burger plus lobster alfredo and chicken parm.

before: Pasta in a bucket. Tuesday special is $7 and feeds two people easily. Garlic bread is huge.
after: Pasta in a bucket. Tuesday special is $7 and supposedly feeds two people easily. Heard the garlic bread is huge.

before: Started the Dallas craft pizza craze. Naples-certified. Try the Honey Bastard pizza.
after: Started the Dallas craft pizza craze. Naples-certified. The Honey Bastard pizza is the one people say to try.
